articles.title: KRAKEN WINS DIRECT ACCESS TO THE FED'S PAYMENT RAILS
articles.summary: Kraken's Wyoming-chartered banking arm has secured a Federal Reserve master account, granting it direct access to Fedwire and allowing it to settle US dollar transfers without intermediary banks. The move marks the first time a crypto-native firm has plugged into the Fed's core payment infrastructure, potentially speeding up fiat flows for institutional clients and strengthening Kraken's positioning ahead of its planned IPO.

articles.title: TETHER INVESTS IN EIGHT SLEEP, PLANS QVAC INTEGRATION
articles.summary: Tether has announced a strategic investment in Eight Sleep, a sleep fitness and health technology company, marking another step in the stablecoin issuer's ongoing diversification beyond its core USDT business. The partnership centers on integrating QVAC, Tether's edge AI platform designed for on-device, locally-run intelligence, into Eight Sleep's product suite and research pipeline. The collaboration positions QVAC as a commercializable layer for personalized health data processing, with Eight Sleep serving as a go-to-market vehicle for the platform's real-world applications in sleep tracking, recovery, and longevity monitoring.

articles.title: ACHIEVING QUANTUM SAFETY THROUGH EPHEMERAL KEY PAIRS
articles.summary: Ethereum wallets can achieve quantum safety without protocol changes by leveraging account abstraction. This design makes ECDSA key pairs single-use, rotating the signer during every transaction while keeping the contract address constant. It eliminates long-term public key exposure, protecting assets from future quantum computing threats.

articles.title: VIRTUALS PROTOCOL DEBUTS FABRIC FOUNDATION'S $ROBO
articles.summary: Virtuals Protocol debuted its Titan launch mechanism – a new tier designed for projects with established scale entering deep public liquidity – through Fabric Foundation's $ROBO token generation event on Base Chain via Uniswap. Fabric, powered by OpenMind, is building a crypto-native payment, identity, and capital allocation layer for physical robots, applying onchain identity and programmable incentives to enable autonomous economic participation by robotic hardware. The protocol's Coordination Pools allow community participants to fund robot fleet deployment using stablecoins, with employers settling robotic labor costs in $ROBO, positioning the token as the network's primary settlement currency. The initial Uniswap V3 pool was seeded with $250K of $VIRTUAL paired against 0.1% of the total $ROBO supply, establishing baseline liquidity at launch.

articles.title: HOW CIRCLE'S NANOPAYMENTS WORK
articles.summary: Circle's Nanopayments enables USDC transfers as small as $0.000001 by having buyers sign offchain EIP-3009 authorization messages routed through the Circle Gateway, which aggregates thousands of those signatures and settles them in a single periodic onchain batch transaction, reducing effective gas costs to near-zero. The buyer's initial deposit is the only onchain transaction, with subsequent payments locked against balance in the Gateway and fulfilled immediately upon signature verification, enabling real-time resource delivery via the x402 protocol. The Circle Gateway operates inside an AWS Nitro Enclave TEE, with batch signing keys protected by AWS KMS and inaccessible to Circle personnel, providing a verifiable trust boundary without requiring a decentralized sequencer.

articles.title: SEC SUBMITS CRYPTO SECURITIES FRAMEWORK TO WHITE HOUSE
articles.summary: The SEC submitted commission-level interpretive guidance to the White House outlining how federal securities laws apply to crypto assets, currently undergoing interagency review at OIRA. The guidance likely focuses on establishing a "token taxonomy" to categorize which crypto assets qualify as securities, affecting how firms register, disclose, and operate. Unlike staff-level statements, commission-level guidance is considered more enforceable and does not require a commission vote. Separately, the CFTC submitted a prediction markets measure to OIRA, with Chairman Selig announcing plans for an advanced notice of proposed rulemaking to set clear standards for event contract markets amid state-federal jurisdictional disputes.

articles.title: CRYPTO CARD SPEND UP AS ADOPTION EXPANDS BEYOND EMERGING MARKETS
articles.summary: Fireblocks' latest Stablecoin Signals highlights that monthly crypto card spend surpassed $115M in January 2025, up from roughly $25M a year earlier, with new players like Ether.fi gaining traction alongside established providers. The data challenges the narrative that crypto cards are confined to developing regions, showing accelerating adoption across EEA countries and rapid growth in Asia, Europe, and Latin America as providers like Gnosis Pay, RedotPay, and Avici Money scale programs.

articles.title: FATF WARNS STABLECOINS DOMINATE ILLICIT ACTIVITY
CFTC CHIEF SELIG TO CLEAR PATH FOR US PERPETUAL FUTURES IN COMING WEEKS
articles.summary: CFTC Chairman Mike Selig announced that US crypto perpetual futures policies will debut within a month.

articles.title: RIPPLE EXPANDS INTO END-TO-END STABLECOIN INFRASTRUCTURE
articles.summary: Ripple expanded its payments platform into a full-stack infrastructure layer, integrating acquisitions Palisade and Rail.

articles.title: BITCOIN NEARS $72K AS SPOT ETF INFLOWS CONTINUE
articles.summary: Bitcoin rallied toward $72,000 as US spot BTC ETF inflows extended into a second session and cushioned upside.

articles.title: COINBASE CEO SAYS BASE APP'S SOCIALFI FEATURES 'DIDN'T QUITE WORK'
articles.summary: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong publicly acknowledged that Base App's SocialFi features "didn't quite work," marking an admission of failure for the L2's consumer social push and signaling a potential strategic pivot for Base's onchain social ambitions.
